Three spheres each of unit radius have centers $P, Q, R$ with the property that
the center of each sphere lies on the surface of the other two spheres. Let $C$
denote the cylinder with cross-section $PQR$ (the triangular lamina with
vertices $P, Q, R$) and axis perpendicular to $PQR$. Let $M$ denote the space
which is common to the three spheres and the cylinder $C$, and suppose the mass
density of $M$ at a given point is the distance of the point from $PQR$.
Determine the mass of $M$.
